findings.title: NVIDIA/TensorRT-LLM
findings.description: TensorRT LLM provides users with an easy-to-use Python API to define Large Language Models (LLMs) and supports state-of-the-art optimizations to perform inference efficiently on NVIDIA GPUs. TensorRT LLM also contains components to create Python and C++ runtimes that orchestrate the inference execution in a performant way.

findings.title: Visplot: A visibility plot and observation scheduling tool for astronomical observatories
findings.abstract: We present Visplot, a free, open-source, web-based tool for hardware-aware visibility analysis and heuristic scheduling of both sidereal and non-sidereal astronomical observations. findings.title: One Token per Highly Selective Frame: Towards Extreme Compression for Long Video Understanding
findings.abstract: Long video understanding is inherently challenging for vision-language models (VLMs) because of the extensive number of frames. With each video frame typically expanding into tens or hundreds of tokens, the limited context length of large language models (LLMs) forces the VLMs to perceive the frames

findings.title: Quantum matter is weakly entangled at low energies
findings.abstract: We construct upper bounds on entanglement entropies of many-body quantum states that have fixed energy expectation values with respect to geometrically local Hamiltonians. Our focus is on entanglement entropies of subsystems that make up approximately half of the full system. The upper bound on the

findings.title: From $P(y|x)$ to $P(y)$: Investigating Reinforcement Learning in Pre-train Space
findings.abstract: While reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances LLM reasoning by optimizing the conditional distribution P(y|x), its potential is fundamentally bounded by the base model's existing output distribution. findings.title: Geometric Context Transformer for Streaming 3D Reconstruction
findings.abstract:
Streaming 3D reconstruction aims to recover 3D information, such as camera poses and point clouds, from a video stream, which necessitates geometric accuracy, temporal

findings.title: From Feelings to Metrics: Understanding and Formalizing How Users Vibe-Test LLMs
findings.abstract: Evaluating LLMs is challenging, as benchmark scores often fail to capture models' real-world usefulness. Instead, users often rely on ``vibe-testing'': informal experience-based evaluation, such as comparing models on coding tasks related to their own workflow. While prevalent, vibe-testing is often

findings.title: Temporary Power Adjusting Withholding Attack
findings.abstract: We consider the block withholding attacks on pools, more specifically the state-of-the-art Power Adjusting Withholding (PAW) attack. We propose a generalization called Temporary PAW (T-PAW) where the adversary withholds a fPoW from pool mining at most $T$-time even when no other block is mined. We s

findings.title: Don't Let the Video Speak: Audio-Contrastive Preference Optimization for Audio-Visual Language Models
findings.abstract: While Audio-Visual Language Models (AVLMs) have achieved remarkable progress over recent years, their reliability is bottlenecked by cross-modal hallucination. A particularly pervasive manifestation is video-driven audio hallucination: models routinely exploit visual shortcuts to hallucinate expecte

findings.title: Rhetorical Questions in LLM Representations: A Linear Probing Study
findings.abstract: Rhetorical questions are asked not to seek information but to persuade or signal stance. How large language models internally represent them remains unclear. We analyze rhetorical questions in LLM representations using linear probes on two social-media datasets with different discourse contexts, and

findings.title: AI-assisted writing and the reorganization of scientific knowledge
findings.abstract: Generative AI systems such as ChatGPT are increasingly used in scientific writing, yet their broader implications for the organization of scientific knowledge remain unclear. We examine whether AI-assisted writing intensity, measured as the share of text in a paper that is predicted to exhibit featu

findings.title: Correct Prediction, Wrong Steps? Consensus Reasoning Knowledge Graph for Robust Chain-of-Thought Synthesis
findings.abstract: LLM reasoning traces suffer from complex flaws -- *Step Internal Flaws* (logical errors, hallucinations, etc.) and *Step-wise Flaws* (overthinking, underthinking), which vary by sample. A natural approach would be to provide ground-truth labels to guide LLMs' reasoning. Contrary to intuition, we sho

findings.title: Single Plane Spatial Mode Sorter
findings.abstract: A mode sorter separates a set of M orthogonal spatial modes in a shared input channel into M different output channels. Here we present an analytic derivation and experimental validation of a single plane device for sorting spatial modes from a diverse variety of mode families, including Hermite-Gau

findings.title: TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration
findings.abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have empowered AI research agents to perform isolated scientific tasks, automating complex, real-world workflows, such as LLM training, remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we introduce TREX, a multi-agent system that automates the entire LLM training li

findings.title: Interpretable Stylistic Variation in Human and LLM Writing Across Genres, Models, and Decoding Strategies
findings.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are now capable of generating highly fluent, human-like text. They enable many applications, but also raise concerns such as large scale spam, phishing, or academic misuse. While much work has focused on detecting LLM-generated text, only limited work has gone into under

findings.title: Momentum Further Constrains Sharpness at the Edge of Stochastic Stability
findings.abstract: Recent work suggests that (stochastic) gradient descent self-organizes near an instability boundary, shaping both optimization and the solutions found. findings.title: Non-symmetric quantum interfaces with bilayer atomic arrays
findings.abstract: We study quantum light-matter interfaces based on bilayer atomic arrays in free space, considering interlayer spacings $a_z$ that may deviate from the Bragg-symmetric condition, $a_z\in \mathrm{integer}\times λ/2$ with $λ$ the light wavelength. Mapping the problem to a one-dimensional model, we show

findings.title: Towards SAFE-ISAC: STAR-RIS-Aided Joint Jamming Suppression and Target Concealment
findings.abstract: Designing robust architectures that can mitigate sophisticated attacks is now a key priority for modern wireless systems. findings.title: Multistage Conditional Compositional Optimization
findings.abstract: We introduce Multistage Conditional Compositional Optimization (MCCO) as a new paradigm for decision-making under uncertainty that combines aspects of multistage stochastic programming and conditional stochastic optimization. MCCO minimizes a nest of conditional expectations and nonlinear cost funct

findings.title: Training-Free Semantic Multi-Object Tracking with Vision-Language Models
findings.abstract: Semantic Multi-Object Tracking (SMOT) extends multi-object tracking with semantic outputs such as video summaries, instance-level captions, and interaction labels, aiming to move from trajectories to human-interpretable descriptions of dynamic scenes. findings.title: Deep Neural Network-guided PSO for Tracking a Global Optimal Position in Complex Dynamic Environment
findings.abstract: We propose novel particle swarm optimization (PSO) variants incorporated with deep neural networks (DNNs) for particles to pursue globally optimal positions in dynamic environments.
